summary: Ferritin is a protein that stores iron in the body. Measuring it is the best single way to assess whether your iron stores are low (a common cause of tiredness) or high.
what_it_measures: The amount of stored iron in the body, measured as the ferritin protein concentration in blood, reported in ug/L (equivalent to ng/mL).

interpretations.meaning: A low ferritin means depleted iron stores and is the most common cause of iron-deficiency anaemia, often presenting as fatigue, breathlessness or pale skin.
A high ferritin can reflect iron overload, but ferritin also rises with inflammation or infection, so it is interpreted alongside other tests.

when_to_seek_help: See your GP if your ferritin is low and you feel persistently tired, or if it is high, as further tests may be needed to find the cause.
approved_by: Demo dataset (illustrative values, not for clinical use)
